zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss於1370年出生在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾度巴頓時，他的父親Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk20歲，母親Helen Erskine25歲。他於大約1385年與Jean Erskine在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾度巴頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1437年被列為member of parliament。他去世於1439年9月24日在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾音姆連享年69歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Colquhoun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Alastair.

Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Colquhoun in Dumbartonshire. The name appears to derive from Gaelic còil, cùil ‘nook, corner’, or coill(e) ‘wood’ + cumhann ‘narrow’. The usual Scottish pronunciation is ka-hoon. This is the name of a Scottish family descended from Umfridus de Kilpatrick, who acquired lands in the barony of Colquhoun during the reign of Alexander II (1214–49).

variants.name: Cohoon
meanings: English (Cheshire) and Scottish: variant of   Colquhoun  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Calhoun
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Colquhoun  .

History: This prominent early American name was brought across the Atlantic Ocean by Scotch-Irish pioneers who entered Pennsylvania c. 1733. The American statesman John c. Calhoun (1782–1850), born in Abbeville District, SC, served in both houses of Congress, was secretary of war, and vice president of the US (1825–32).

variants.name: Kahoun
meanings: Irish: variant of Scottish   Colquhoun  .

variants.name: Cahoon
meanings: Irish (Down and Antrim): variant (reflecting the traditional pronunciation) of Scottish   Colquhoun  .

variants.name: Calhoon
meanings: Irish: variant of Scottish   Colquhoun  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

variants.name: Lente
meanings: Native American (Pueblo) and Hispanic: nickname from Spanish lente ‘lens’. This surname originated among the Pueblos in NM.
German: variant of   Lenth  .
Portuguese: habitational name from Lente, in Porto district.

variants.name: Lent
meanings: Dutch (Van Lent): habitational name from Lent in Elst, Gelderland, meaning a place where lime trees grew.
German: variant of   Lenth  .

nameMeanings.name: Luss
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Kent): from the Old Swedish personal name Guse, Gusse.
South German (Güss): topographic name for someone who lived near a torrent or on a flood plain, from Middle High German güsse ‘flood, flooding’. Compare Guess .

variants.name: Guess
meanings: English (southern): probably a variant of Guest with loss of final -t.
South German (Güss): see Guss .
German: variant of   Geis  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss was born in 1370, in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, his father, Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk, was 20 and his mother, Helen Erskine, was 25. He married Jean Erskine about 1385, in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. In 1437, at the age of 67, his occupation is listed as member of parliament. He died on 24 September 1439, in Inchmurrin, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Luss, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss est né en 1370, à Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, son père, Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Helen Erskine, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Jean Erskine environ 1385, à Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. En 1437, à l’âge de 67 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1439, à Inchmurrin, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Luss, Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss 1370 in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk 20 und seine Mutter Helen Erskine 25. Er hat um 1385 in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland Jean Erskine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1437, als er 67 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als member of parliament aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. September 1439 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Inchmurrin, Dunbartonshire, Scotland und wurde in Luss, Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss è nato il nel 1370 nel Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Helen Erskine, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Jean Erskine circa 1385, nel Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1437, all'età di 67 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament. Morì il 24 settembre 1439, nel Inchmurrin, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Luss, Argyll and Bute, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Lussが1370年にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアダンバートンで生まれた時，父Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilkは20歳，母Helen Erskineは25歳であった。約1385年にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアダンバートンでJean Erskineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1437年，67歳の時，職業はmember of parliamentと記録されている。彼は1439年9月24日にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアインチムリンで69歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss(이)가 1370년 스컷랜드 던바토실르 드바턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Helen Erskine(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1385년 스컷랜드 던바토실르 드바턴에서 Jean Erskine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1437년, 67세의 나이로 그의 직업은 member of parliament이었다. 그는 1439년 9월 24일 스컷랜드 던바토실르 인ch머린에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 루스에 묻혔다.

pt.Luss, Loch Lomond, Scotland: Luss, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.Luss Parish Churchyard, Luss, Argyll and: Luss, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss nasceu em 1370, em Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, seu pai, Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Helen Erskine, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Jean Erskine aproximadamente 1385, em Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1437, com 67 anos, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament. Ele faleceu em 24 de setembro de 1439, em Inchmurrin, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Luss, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Luss Parish Churchyard, Luss, Argyll and: лусс, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss родился в 1370 г. в местности думбартон, дунбартоншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen Erskine, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Jean Erskine около 1385 г. в местности думбартон, дунбартоншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. В 1437 году, в возрасте 67 лет,  он работал по профессии member of parliament. Он умер 24 сентября 1439 г. в местности инчмуррин, дунбартоншир, Шотландия в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности лусс, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.Luss Parish Churchyard, Luss, Argyll and: Luss, Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Colquhoun - Eighth Lord of Colquhoun, Tenth Lord of Luss nació en 1370, en Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, su papá, Humphrey Colquhoun 6th Colquhoun of that Ilk, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Helen Erskine, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Jean Erskine aproximadamente 1385, en Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. En 1437, a la edad de 67 años, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament. Murió el 24 de septiembre de 1439, en Inchmurrin, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Luss, Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido.
